strengths: Exceeds minimum experience requirement by 4 years (19+ years vs. 15 required); demonstrates sustained technical seniority through multiple staff-level positions
Currently working on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling at re:cinq, showing recent direct engagement with AI technology platforms
Proven platform builder with open-source GISCollective project and track record of building distributed systems handling millions of requests; demonstrates technical depth across domains
concerns: AI experience limited to 2 months (April 2025-present); no prior AI/ML roles, no demonstrated products or platforms built with AI—critical gap against required 'recognized expertise in AI with demonstrated products'
No Master's degree or PhD in AI-related field (preferred qualification); absence particularly significant given shallow AI background to date
No evidence of strategic advisory work advising customers or partners on cloud/AI adoption; background is hands-on engineering rather than director-level strategic positioning

key_factors.factor: Total years experience vs. minimum requirement

response.justification: The candidate meets the minimum 15-year requirement decisively (19+ years) and demonstrates strong technical seniority through multiple staff-level roles. However, the role's core focus on 'AI Strategy' requires 'recognized expertise in AI with demonstrated products or platforms built with AI.' The candidate's AI experience dates to April 2025 (MCP infrastructure tooling only), with no prior AI/ML background, no AI products, and no strategic advisory track record with partners. While the current MCP role signals recent AI engagement, it is insufficient for a director-level specialist role tasked with identifying strategic AI opportunities and advising on cutting-edge adoption. The absence of an advanced AI degree compounds this gap. The candidate is a strong platform builder but lacks the specialized AI expertise and strategic positioning this role demands.
